_comment: Workbook config: sheet count, sheet types, theming, and general instructions. User input is assumed to have all required inputs.

sheet_format_defaults._comment: How many sheets and their types/definitions

sheets.name: Inputs
sheets.description: Informative display of assumption parameters: base values, YoY growth, ranges, and rationale. Not a single-column list—show growth rates, year-over-year values, range (min-max), and rationale where available.

structure.columns: Label | Base Value | YoY Growth (%) | Range | Rationale/Notes; for time-varying params use year columns with values per year

notes: Make Inputs sheet informative: include Base Value, YoY Growth (%), Range where available. For params with yoyGrowth or growth_rate, show both value and growth. Add Rationale/Notes column when present in assumptions. Fill actual years/months in column headers. No single-column layout—use multiple columns per parameter.

sheets.name: Calculations
sheets.description: Contains forecast calculations referencing the Inputs sheet. Use formulas to calculate values based on assumptions.

structure.columns: years or months based on granularity

sections: All the columns and rows from the Inputs sheet
Added calucalted cells at each iteration
notes: import all values from the input sheet and reference them within the caluculation sheet

sheets.name: Summary
sheets.description: Key outputs only. Reference or pull values from the Calculations sheet; no intermediate formulas, only final calculated values.

structure.columns: years or months based on granularity

notes: Populate from Calculations. If multiple 0s or #REF!/#DIV/0! appear, fix upstream references.

single_sheet_option.name: Forecast Model
single_sheet_option.description: All-in-one sheet with inputs and calculations combined

general_instructions: Patient funnel — iterative chain matching forecast_server.py compute_forecast exactly: (1) Total Population = base_population × (1 + pop_growth)^year_index; (2) Affected = Total Population × prevalence_or_incidence_rate; (3) Diagnosed = Affected × diagnosis_rate; (4) Eligible = Diagnosed × eligibility_rate; (5) Eligible_for_treat = Eligible × severity_rate [if severity selected, else same as Eligible]; (6) Treated = Eligible_for_treat × treatment_rate; (7) Treated_net = Treated × (1 − progression_rate) [if progression selected, else same as Treated]; (8) Class Patients = Treated_net × class_share (S-curve normalized logistic k=5); (9) Product Patients = Class Patients × product_share (S-curve); (10) Gross Sales ($M) = Product Patients × annual_cost_per_patient / 1,000,000; (11) Net Sales ($M) = Gross Sales × (1 − discount_rate). Peak Net Sales ($M) = MAX of Net Sales across all forecast years. CRITICAL: Class Patients multiplies Treated_net (step 7), NOT Eligible (step 4) — this must be correct in all Excel formulas. Forecast period: Must include launch_year and peak_year (inclusive). Use peak_year − launch_year + 1 when both present; else follow forecast_period_resolution.

forecast_period_resolution._comment: Forecast must include launch_year and peak_year (inclusive). Resolve in this order.
forecast_period_resolution.rules: 1) Check user input for forecast_period. If present, ensure it covers launch through peak. 2) If launch_year and peak_year both present: forecast_period = peak_year - launch_year + 1 (inclusive). 3) Else if time_to_peak (adoptionPeakTime.value, classShare.timeToPeak, peakProductShare.timeToPeak) present, use as forecast_period. 4) Else default 10.
